name: Guideline on Technical Measures for Ensuring AI Security
owner: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications, Japan (MIC)
description: Finalized (not still draft) after public comment closed January 29, 2026 (52 comments received). Focuses on technical measures against AI-specific threats: unauthorized/malicious output generation, system malfunction, and confidential-information leakage via AI system manipulation.
relationToCloudSecurity: See registry/control/soumu.go.jp.json in the SecID repo (CloudSecurityAlliance/SecID) for cloud/AI security relevance notes on this source.
relationToAISecurity: See the paired SecID registry entry.

license.notes: Issued directly by a national government ministry/agency as an official notification/circular -- plausibly excluded from copyright under Japan's Copyright Act Article 13(2) ("notifications, instructions, circulars, and other documents similar thereto issued by national or local government organs"), but this is a less clear-cut case than primary legislation (Art. 13(1)) and has not been legally verified. Treat as moderate-confidence permitted, not certain.

current_extraction.notes: Only the announcement page confirming finalization was acquired (Japanese; Shift-JIS encoded, re-decoded correctly after an initial garbled attempt). Direct links to the actual guideline body PDF were not found in the page's static HTML (likely constructed via JS or a search portal) -- the guideline's substantive technical content is not yet acquired.

scope.notes: Announcement page only. Guideline body PDF not yet located/acquired.
